reader: AI engineer who understands Code Agents but is not yet fluent in robotics control
changes: added a reader-lens section to clarify the intended reading perspective
added definitions for policy code, Action Chunk, and visual servoing
added pseudocode showing how policy code can produce a constrained Action Chunk
recorded cleanup candidates in to-delete.md instead of deleting placeholders
followups: split the visual servoing benchmark into its own idea page
expand the pseudocode into a more concrete interface contract
decide which placeholder portal pages should be kept or cleaned after human review

reader: first-time technical reader who needs the thesis before the full argument
changes: added a 30-second read section that states the claim, mechanism, and boundary of the route
followups: turn the quick read into a reusable pattern for future idea pages

reader: systems engineer who wants clear responsibility boundaries before believing the route
changes: added a four-layer model separating task understanding, policy code, action interfaces, and execution feedback
followups: expand the action interface layer into an interface contract page

reader: skeptical robotics reader comparing direct action policies with code-mediated control
changes: added a comparison table contrasting direct Action Chunk output with policy-code-generated Action Chunks across explainability, supervision, and transfer
followups: add concrete examples of failure cases where direct Action Chunks are hard to debug

reader: safety-minded operator who wants to know why longer model thinking is acceptable
changes: expanded the safe-task argument into an observe-plan-check-short-execute-review rhythm
followups: connect the safety rhythm to concrete stop conditions and intervention signals

reader: robotics platform engineer evaluating whether modular code blocks map onto real hardware subsystems
changes: added a module collaboration table for left hand, head observation, and right-hand sensing blocks
followups: add a signal vocabulary for pause, retry, reframe, and abort messages

reader: software architect concerned that parallel robot modules need a simple coordination protocol
changes: added a signal vocabulary for pause, retry, reframe, confirm, and abort between local code blocks
followups: show a concrete event loop for module coordination

reader: benchmark designer looking for concrete visual servoing tasks to evaluate
changes: added a visual servoing benchmark grid covering translation centering, rotation to side, occlusion recovery, and multi-view consistency
followups: turn the benchmark grid into a dedicated scoring rubric

reader: ML researcher asking what additional supervision code generation makes possible
changes: added a supervision ladder from task success through intermediate states, constraints, and transferability of code
followups: define measurable rewards for each supervision level

reader: reviewer focused on what errors become easier to detect with policy code
changes: added a failure taxonomy covering wrong targets, missing constraints, insufficient observation, and simulation shortcuts
followups: add examples of automatic penalties for each failure type

reader: technical reviewer who needs a practical pre-execution audit checklist
changes: added a policy-code audit checklist for goals, observation gaps, constraints, recovery choices, and simulation shortcuts
followups: turn the checklist into a reusable review template for proposals
